title: Sour Crispy Russian Cabbage Sauerkraut
description: A traditional Russian dish featuring sour fermented cabbage, crispy texture, and energizing flavors from basil. Diabetic-friendly and perfect for a snack or side dish.
instructions: Shred the cabbage and place it in a large bowl.
Add the caraway seeds, black pepper, salt, and chopped basil to the bowl and mix well.
In a separate bowl, mix the water and apple cider vinegar together.
Pour the water-vinegar mixture over the cabbage mixture and mix until everything is well coated.
Let the mixture sit at room temperature (about 70-75°F) for 3-5 days, allowing it to ferment.
After 3-5 days, use a mandoline or sharp knife to shred the cabbage mixture into thin strips.
Place the shredded cabbage mixture in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Dehydrate the cabbage mixture in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2-3 hours, or until crispy.
Check the cabbage for crispiness by removing a piece from the oven and letting it cool. If it's still soft, continue dehydrating for another 30 minutes.
Once crispy, remove the cabbage from the oven and let it cool completely.
Store the sour crispy cabbage in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
